10718.author: cms-sw
10718.body:
Python 3.12.13 along with massive update for python modules
- xz 5.8.3 (which rebuilds python3 so we propose to update python version + python modules)
- pip 26.1.2
- setuptools 83.0.0

As this rebuild nearly all the externals (due to rebuild of python3) so I also propose to update the build configuration of externals to use CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=%{cmake_build_type} which will allow us to configure and build a package in debug mode using cmsBuild --define cms_debug_packages=pkg1,pkg2
10718.title: Python 3.12.13, massive update of python modules

49637.author: Chihwan-An
49637.body:
Added Single Tau Trigger path for Phase2 , worked with Tau HLT Phase2 development group.

It was tested in miniAOD in CMSSW_15_1_0_pre4 and NanoAOD in CMSSW_16_0_0_pre2 , with Z'to tautau samples.

Trigger Efficiency had 48.7% .


* #49569 was submitted before.
    - MinEta for HLT1PFTau EDFilters has a default value of -1.0, which the config understands it as being -2.1. 
* [Slides](https://mattermost.web.cern.ch/files/w9xa1zkcjtf48ejuqi1etq1dgw/public?h=MJCc9wYFJm_ItqMkJMVt0hFs5H5-Zy2r11s6_XBi5lo) are not shared with TSG group yet , shared with Tau HLT phase2 group .
49637.title: Phase2 Single_Tau_Trigger Path Added

50269.author: bfonta
50269.body:
Co-authored by @Parsifal-2045.

This PR includes a series of improvements on `dqm-plot`:
+ support user-defined legend title
+ support `tex`-formatted energy labels
+ support user-defined axis limits
+ user-defined x and y axis titles
+ removes a "'\m escape sequence' warning."
+ use the full variable name as the save name to avoid ambiguities (occasionally when specifying a single variable to plot, all variables containing that substring would also be plotted, and with a different name)
+ introduce sequential processing to help debugging
+ add an additional "pT" tex string to ensure log scale is correctly applied by default to all pT distributions
+ support different user-defined font sizes
+ support user-defined plot title
+ introduce a system to apply some simple operations for convenience: i implemented the division operation, but others can be easily added in the future. These operations are useful when the DQM validation step is still missing some important derivable quantity, as for instance the scale-normalized response.
+  introduce a "complement" operation, which performs the "1-x" operation; useful to convert for instance from a purity measurement to a fake rate one
+ support histogram rebinning, both with a fixed number of bins, or by providing user-defined bin edges
+ support the multiplication of values along the x axis by a fixed constant. Useful for unit conversion.
+ drop the unused overlay_groups parameter of plot_comparison
+ default the ratio label to "Ratio"; "--ratio-label" or "--ratio-label auto" give "Ratio wrt <first legend label>", and "--ratio-label VALUE" sets a custom label. 
+ improved skipped histogram warning messages
+ decide each object's type from the class name stored in its key instead of reading the object: only directories are deserialized and histograms are never read during the scan, leading to a ~2.7x speed-up.
+ optimize _load_single_histogram to keep opened files in cache
+ extend the --overlay option to support specific collection@file pairs; useful when the user wants to compare different collections across multiple files
+ support user-defined legend labels and legend title when using the --overlay option
+ support user-defined legend location
+ support y error clamping as a quick replacement to Clopper-Pearson uncertainties

The code has been thoroughly tested in a variety of scenarios.

----

For the future, we are still missing an implementation assuming the sources contain the full directory structure of the DQM files, in order to only traverse the directories containing the histograms to be plotted, providing a potentially significant speed-up increase for when the user only wants to plot a specific variable. Given recent work on `dqm-plot`, this feature became less pressing; I will keep it for later.
50269.title: dqm-plot improvements

50842.author: fwyzard
50842.body:
#### PR description:

Implement an utility to resolve a C++ type name to its actual definition, taking into account type aliases and the use of `ALPAKA_ACCELERATOR_NAMESPACE`, and print the full c++ name, the friendly class name, and the mangled name.

#### PR validation:

Validate the new script with a dedicated unit test.
50842.title: Implement `edmTypeInfo`

50888.author: Electricks94
50888.body:
This PR extends the out-of-range error messages that are thrown in the SoA backend. The messages are extended by the file, in which the error is triggered, and the line number. This is done by using `std::source_location`. For example, an error message before this PR might look like this:

`Out of range index in ConstViewTemplateFreeParams TrackingHitsLayout::operator[]: index 7 out of range 2`

The same error message would now look like this, for example:

`Out of range index in ConstViewTemplateFreeParams TrackingHitsLayout::operator[]: index 3 out of range 2 at file src/RecoTracker/PixelSeeding/plugins/alpaka/CAPixelDoubletsAlgos.h line 247`

With this extension it is immediately clear where the problem is triggered, which helps for debugging faster.


@felicepantaleo fyi
50888.title: Add source location to SoA out of range error messages

51084.author: jchismar
51084.body: Implementation of a track purity DNN used for high purity selection for HLT tracks. Initial results were presented at the [tracking POG meeting on 15 Dec 2025](https://indico.cern.ch/event/1622188/#82-using-a-dnn-for-the-quality). Since then, the model has been retrained with the latest version of LST, and a separate threshold has been implemented for displaced tracks (|dxy| > 0.5) to improve displaced track efficiency. This threshold is set at a target efficiency of 99.5% calculated on tracks with |dxy| > 0.5. For tracks with |dxy| $\le$ 0.5, the threshold is set at a target efficiency of 99.5% calculated on all tracks. Additionally, the number of input features has been reduced from 29 to 15 with no loss of performance. The DNN is configured to run in the HLTInitialStepSequence after the hltInitialStepTracks step when the trackTorchClassifier procModifier is used. MTV performance on TT+PU=200 is shown below.
51084.title: Track Purity DNN for Phase-2 HLT

51085.author: JanGerritSchulz
51085.body:
#### PR description:

This PR was co-developed by: @Parsifal-2045

This pull request introduces some changes and updates to the Patatrack pixel tracking targetting Phase-2:
- some new cuts and settings for Patatrack including a cut on the quadruplet-level in track building and ability to disable the various duplicate treatments in the CA
- restructuring and improvements to the duplicate treatment leading to better efficiency at the cost of increased duplicate rate
- tuning of the CA parameters to collect more hits in the barrel region

The improvements were discussed in a similar version to what is proposed here in a working meeting of the Tracking POG [here](https://indico.cern.ch/event/1657943/#2-updates-on-extended-ca-in-ph).

Note that this PR is effectively coupled with #51042 which introduces a DNN pixel track selection directly on the device. This will lead to an improved efficiency due to smarter fake removal and at the same time reduced fake rate. This PR instead increases the track quality and initial efficiency before track selection. The full improvement is hidden in the usual MTV plots as the current track selection gets rid most of the additional signal tracks. As a side effect the generally loser doublet and triplet cuts also increase the fake rate. The overall combined effect with #51042 was presented [here](https://indico.cern.ch/event/1680142/#92-update-on-pixel-track-dnn-a)

#### PR validation:

Running tracking validation for Run-3 and Phase-2 using the commands below leads to the results here:
- [Run-3 performance](https://jaschulz.web.cern.ch/Plots/NGT/PR_tests/2026_May_PatatrackDevelopments/TrackingValidation_Run3_TTbar_PU/)
- [Phase-2 performance](https://jaschulz.web.cern.ch/Plots/NGT/PR_tests/2026_May_PatatrackDevelopments/TrackingValidation_Phase2_TTbar_PU/)

Note: *The Run-3 validation shows some differences in the performance, however since this PR will enter CMSSW_20_X that should be irrelevant for the integration.*

**Run-3 validation:**
```bash
#!/bin/bash -ex

cmsDriver.py step2 -s HLT:@relval2026,VALIDATION:hltMultiTrackValidation \
         --conditions auto:phase1_2026_realistic \
         --datatier DQMIO \
         -n -1 \
         --eventcontent DQMIO \
         --geometry DB:Extended \
         --era Run3_2026 \
         --filein file:/eos/cms/store/relval/CMSSW_17_0_0_pre1/RelValTTbar_14TeV/GEN-SIM-DIGI-RAW/PU_160X_mcRun3_2026_realistic_v6_STD_2026_RegeneratedGS_PU_20260519_170938-v2/2590000/0227c321-792a-4aa0-8d5c-55d96a5e5943.root  \
         --fileout file:step2.root \
         --nThreads 24 \
         --process HLTX \
         --inputCommands='keep *, drop *_hlt*_*_HLT, drop triggerTriggerFilterObjectWithRefs_l1t*_*_HLT' \
         > step2.log  2>&1

cmsDriver.py step3 -s HARVESTING:postProcessorHLTtrackingSequence \
         --conditions auto:phase1_2026_realistic  \
         --mc \
         --geometry DB:Extended \
         --scenario pp \
         --filetype DQM \
         --era Run3_2026 \
         -n 1000  \
         --filein file:step2.root \
         --fileout file:step3.root > step3.log  2>&1
```

**Phase-2 validation:**
```bash
#!/bin/bash -ex

cmsDriver.py step_2 -s L1P2GT,HLT:75e33_trackingOnly,VALIDATION:@hltValidation \
         --mc \
         --conditions auto:phase2_realistic_T35 \
         --datatier DQMIO \
         -n -1 \
         --eventcontent DQMIO \
         --geometry ExtendedRun4D121 \
         --era Phase2C17I13M9 \
         --customise SLHCUpgradeSimulations/Configuration/aging.customise_aging_1000 \
         --filein file:/eos/cms/store/relval/CMSSW_16_1_0_pre2/RelValTTbar_14TeV/GEN-SIM-DIGI-RAW/PU_150X_mcRun4_realistic_v1_STD_D121_RegeneratedGS_PU-v1/2590000/02e107db-90d4-4d51-898a-1d0ae68ee54b.root \
         --fileout file:step2.root \
         --nThreads 24 \
         --process HLTX \
         --inputCommands='keep *, drop *_hlt*_*_HLT, drop triggerTriggerFilterObjectWithRefs_l1t*_*_HLT' \
         > step2.log  2>&1

cmsDriver.py step3 -s HARVESTING:postProcessorHLTtrackingSequence \
         --conditions auto:phase2_realistic_T35  \
         --mc \
         --geometry ExtendedRun4D121 \
         --scenario pp \
         --filetype DQM \
         --era Run3_2026 \
         -n 1000  \
         --filein file:step2.root \
         --fileout file:step3.root > step3.log  2>&1
```

#### If this PR is a backport please specify the original PR and why you need to backport that PR. If this PR will be backported please specify to which release cycle the backport is meant for:

Not a backport.
51085.title: [NGT] Improvements to Patatrack Pixel Tracking for Phase-2

51374.author: xabiercidvidal
51374.body:
#### PR description:

This PR refactors RecoMTD/TrackExtender/plugins/TrackExtenderWithMTD.cc by extracting three utility types that were previously defined in a local anonymous namespace into proper shared headers under RecoMTD/TimingTools/interface/, in the mtd:: namespace:

MTDHitMatchingInfo > stores per-hit geometric and time-compatibility chi values used during MTD hit matching, including the combined sorting criterion
TrackSegments > accumulates per-track path-length segments and sigma-tof contributions for TOF PID computation
TrackTofPidInfo > holds the per-hypothesis (/K/p) TOF PID output (t_mtd, sigma_t_mtd, tof_pi/K/p, sigma_tof_pi/K/p)
The logic of all three types is identical to the original anonymous-namespace definitions; only the enclosing scope and header location change. TrackExtenderWithMTD.cc is updated to include the new headers and drop the local definitions.

More details in [MTD DPG meeting](https://indico.cern.ch/event/1695883/contributions/7131847/attachments/3299549/5901867/MTD-TrackExtender-Refactor_XCV.pdf)

##### Expected output changes: none:  
this is a pure code-organisation refactoring. Validated by running the full Phase-2 MTD DQM workflow (step3 RECO+DQM + step4 harvest, D121 no-PU TTbar, 900 events) and confirming bit-for-bit identical histograms vs. the unmodified baseline.

##### Dependencies: none.

#####  Related PRs: 
this PR is the common base for three follow-up PRs (submitted separately):

mtd-pr2-timing-info: replaces 20 separate edm::ValueMap products with a single reco::MTDTimingInfo object
mtd-pr3-hit-matcher: extracts MTDHitMatcher as a standalone utility class
mtd-pr4-path-length: extracts path-length and TOF utilities into RecoMTD/TimingTools


#### PR validation:

Full Phase-2 simulation chain validated locally on CMSSW_20_1_X (branch rebased onto master tip 815a9fd69c8, 2026-06-29):

(RECO + DQM): 9 jobs  100 events, Phase-2 D121 geometry, no pileup, TTbar input from DigiRaw
(DQM harvesting): hadd of 9 step3 DQM files + cmsRun harvesting step
Comparison of all MTD histograms in the harvested DQM ROOT file between this PR and the baseline (unmodified TrackExtenderWithMTD.cc) using per-bin TH1::GetBinContent() differences: zero bin differences across all 827 MTD-specific histograms.

>> NEW: validation now performed on ttbar sample with PU 200. All still good, plots available [here](https://cernbox.cern.ch/s/Dsezxpah8sseHeU)

>> NEW: RelMon comparison for validation (bin-to-bin test, threshold 0.99999, MTD histograms): 826/827 passing (99.88%)

Full report: https://cernbox.cern.ch/s/ob6ynB5kYwUmwrA

The single failure (TrackSigmaTof_Proton) seems not a physics regression. 9 tracks (0.00048% of 1,886,130) shift between adjacent 1-ps bins at the 29 ps boundary (9 in bin [28,29) ps, +9 in bin [29,30) ps). The total integral is conserved. Evidence:

- TrackSigmaTof_Pion and TrackSigmaTof_Kaon are bit-for-bit identical  (effect isolated to the proton mass term)
- This PR moves TrackSegments from an anonymous-namespace definition inside TrackExtenderWithMTD.cc to a named-namespace header. The formula is unchanged (verified by diff); the compiler makes different inlining decisions, reordering floating point operations by 10 fs. 

>> NEW: timing checks done. 
- RECO (DQM output disabled) was run on a PU 200 sample containing 10 events in baseline and this PR. 
- The machine was lxplus977, AMD EPYC-Genoa, 16 cores, 57 GB RAM. 
- The timing was computed using CMSSW Timing service, per-module wall seconds summed over events via TimeModule> lines; JobCPU from TimeReport> at job end; 
- differences 5% are likely noise on a shared node

Config | JobCPU | trackExtenderWithMTD
-- | -- | --
baseline | 39.0s | 6.42s
pr | 37.9s | 6.67s



#### If this PR is a backport please specify the original PR and why you need to backport that PR. If this PR will be backported please specify to which release cycle the backport is meant for:

Not a backport. No backport intended at this time.
51374.title: RecoMTD: extract TrackSegments into shared TimingTools header

51395.author: Parsifal-2045
51395.body:
#### PR description:

This PR adds tooling to estimate the worst-case CUDA local-memory backing store a `cmsRun` job reserves, and adds related information to the CUDA device information printed by the `CUDAService`.

When a CUDA kernel has a large per-thread stack frame (deep call graphs, register spills, ) the driver reserves a local-memory backing store of  `cudaLimitStackSize  maxResidentThreads` bytes per CUDA context, where `maxResidentThreads = multiProcessorCount  maxThreadsPerMultiProcessor`. The stack limit must cover the largest per-thread frame among the kernels that launch, so this reservation can silently consume a large slice of device memory. 
This PR introduces a new tool to make that budget visible. More details can be found in the 29/06 GPU meeting discussion ([link](https://indico.cern.ch/event/1702667/#8-approaches-to-reduce-kernel))

##### Main developments

1. `cudaComputeCapabilities` `--verbose`/`-v` and `--help`/`-h` 
    - `-v` prints detailed per-device properties (PCI id, memory sizes, clocks, warp size, max threads per multiprocessor, max resident threads, block/grid dimensions, ECC, cooperative launch, ). The default one-line-per-device output is unchanged, so existing consumers are unaffected.

2. `CUDAService` verbose output
    - Adds max threads per multiprocessor, max resident threads, and max resident threads
per CUDA core to the detailed device dump.

3. `cudaKernelStackBudget`: new standalone tool (inspired by `cudaComputeCapabilities`
    - Given a `cmsRun` config it reports `maxResidentThreads  max(STACK)` per device. There are two operating modes:
        - Static mode (default): resolves the config's scheduled modules (`@alpaka` and legacy `cms::cuda`) to their plugin libraries via the plugin cache, reads per-kernel stack frames from those libraries with `cuobjdump --dump-resource-usage`, and reports the largest. This is a conservative upper bound over every kernel in the loaded CUDA libraries (no actual information on what will be run by any given configuration)
        - `--launched` mode: runs `cmsRun` once with the new logger service attached and reports only the kernels that actually launched, attributing each to the module that launched it, alongside the static upper bound.
    - By default only reports the biggest STACK spill, passing `-v`/`--verbose` lists the top 25 spilling kernels. One can additionally pass `--top N` to show more or less of the list (`--top` 0 shows all) 
    - Device numbers auto-detect from `cudaComputeCapabilities --verbose`, or are supplied with `--compute-capability` / `--max-resident-threads` for a no-GPU estimate.

4. `CuptiKernelLoggerService`: new EDM service
    - Subscribes to the CUPTI launch callbacks and records each launched kernel together with the module running on the calling thread, writing a kernel log and the loaded-library list at end-of-job.
    - CUPTI allows one subscriber per process, so if another CUPTI client (`NVProfilerService`, `nsys`, `ncu`) is active it logs a clear warning explaining why the logs will be empty.

#### PR validation:

This is a technical change that has no effects on physics.
The code compiles and runs as expected 
- `CUDAService` and `cudaComputeCapabilities` show the newly added values in verbose mode
- `cudaKernelStackBudget` works as expected both in the default static mode and in `--launched` mode
- New unit test `testKernelStackBudget.py` (29 tests) covers the cuobjdump / device-info / plugin-cache parsers, ELF fatbin detection, CLI splitting and validation, arch selection, the report in static and `--launched` modes, module attribution, and an end-to-end `main()` dummy run. Runs CPU-only (no GPU/`cuobjdump` required)

For reference, I report here a couple of outputs from the kernel running on typical Phase-2 HLT config files:
- static mode (verbose):
```
CUDA kernel stack-frame memory budget
  scheduled modules using CUDA libraries: 5
  CUDA libraries from the configuration:  3
  kernels inspected:                      1212

device 0: NVIDIA H100 NVL (compute capability 9.0, sm_90)
  max resident threads: 270336
  worst-case reservation: 1111.7 MiB (1165688832 B) = 270336 threads x 4312 B
      largest stack frame 4312 B in pluginRecoTrackerPixelSeedingPortableCudaAsync.so

    STACK   4312 B  LOCAL      0 B  REG 255  alpaka_cuda_async::Kernel_CircleFit<5, pixelTopology::HIonPhase1>
```

51425.author: ghyls
51425.body:
#### PR description:

The MPIController calls `beginJob()` only after the constructors of all modules
(including MPIController itself) have completed in the local process. If any
module in the local proces crashes in its constructor, MPIController's
`beginJob()` is never called.

On the remote process, however, MPISource waits for this `beginJob()`
(specifically for the "EDM_MPI_Connect" message) in its constructor.

If any module in the local process crashes at construction time (e.g. because of
config parse errors), the FW calls the destructor of every module, including the
`MPIController`. In this case `beginJob()` never ran, so the Source's
constructor blocks forever. The destructor of `MPIController` calls
`MPI_Comm_disconnect()`, which will also blocks forever since the source hasn't
even got past its constructor.

And the original exception is never printed.

The fix is to track if `beginJob()` completed. If it did, skip
`MPI_Comm_disconnect()` in MPIController's destructor, so that the original
error can be printed.


#### PR validation:

Existing tests pass.
No new tests have been added.
51425.title: Prevent MPI deadlock after a configuration error

51466.author: bfonta
51466.body: Add $p_{\text{T}}(\tau)$ and $|\eta(\tau)|$ pre-selections to the tau validation at generation and reconstruction level. This is achieved by filtering all generated and reconstructed tau collections. The cuts are made configurable. --------- Minor updates: + make it clear that `wpValuesForTau[i]` stores booleans (one per WP). + use `else if` instead of `if` to clarify that `use_wp` and `use_raw` cannot be active at the same time + general declutter by using a few references ------------------ PR tested locally with a few events:
51466.title: Add pre-selections to the Tau Validation.

51467.author: felicepantaleo
51467.body: Two hitMap.find results were dereferenced without an end() check, unlike every sibling lookup in the file. When RecHitMapProducer takes its empty-map fallback (hit collections unavailable) the validator segfaults on the end iterator. Skip such hits, marking them as not linked to any CaloParticle.
51467.title: Guard unguarded hitMap lookups in HGVHistoProducerAlgo

51468.author: felicepantaleo
51468.body: The per-axis radius estimate is a variance over the cluster cells, computed in float against the stored log-weighted cluster position. For clusters whose cells all share one coordinate (e.g. a vertical column of hexagonal silicon cells, about 2500 per TTbar PU200 event) the true variance along that axis is zero and the one-pass formula subtracts two equal rounding residues, landing below zero about half the time; the sqrt then yields a NaN radius whose comparisons are silently false in the seed and density decisions.
51468.title: Clamp the CLUE3D cluster radius variance at zero

51470.author: felicepantaleo
51470.title: TruthInfo: pileup-aware truth graph during mixing

51473.author: raoatifshad
51473.body:
#### PR description:
This PR removes unnecessary static storage from the local vstart and vstep arrays in LASBarrelAlgorithm::CalculateParameters(). These arrays are initialized on every invocation and are only used within the function scope.
function 'LASBarrelAlgorithm::CalculateParameters'
accesses or modifies non-const static local variable '_vstart'.
```[cms.FunctionChecker]
Non-const variable 'float[52] _vstart'
is static local or static member data and might be thread-unsafe
[threadsafety.StaticLocal]
function 'LASBarrelAlgorithm::CalculateParameters'
accesses or modifies non-const static local variable '_vstep'.
[cms.FunctionChecker]
Non-const variable 'float[52] _vstep'
is static local or static member data and might be thread-unsafe
[threadsafety.StaticLocal]
```
[logs here](https://cmssdt.cern.ch/SDT/jenkins-artifacts/ib-static-analysis/CMSSW_20_1_X_2026-07-10-1100/el9_amd64_gcc13/build-logs/Alignment/LaserAlignment/log.html)

Does this approach look acceptable to you, or would you prefer a different solution (e.g. keeping them static const or constexpr)?
51473.title: [WarningFix]Alignment/LaserAlignment: remove unnecessary static local

51476.author: thomreis
51476.body:
#### PR description:

Update ECAL Phase 2 sample noise correlations from 2023 TB measurement. Since only the gain 10 correlations were measured from the 2023 TB data the gain 1 correlations are duplicated from the gain 10 ones until properly measured correlations from more recent TBs become available.

Only ECAL Phase 2 development workflows are affected by this change. No changes are expected to standard workflows.

#### PR validation:

Passes 34402.612, 34634.61299.
51476.title: Update ECAL Phase 2 sample noise correlations

51478.author: mroguljic
51478.body:
#### PR description:

This is a followup to https://github.com/cms-sw/cmssw/pull/51379. That PR resolved cases where some hits in inactive modules would end up incorrectly marked as missing when refitting tracks. Even after this fix, refitted tracks in muon ALCARECOs would end up with more missing / inactive L1 hit patterns than what is stored in the reco step. The affected stored reco tracks had no L1 hit patterns at all.

The tracks associated with this behavior come from `muonSeededStepOutIn` tracking step. In setSecondHitPattern(), the outIn-aware dirForInnerLayers / dirForOuterLayers are correctly used for the compatibleLayers() topological search, but the following compatibleDets() geometric check hardcodes oppositeToMomentum / alongMomentum instead of reusing them. For OutIn tracks this searches for a compatible module in the wrong physical direction, so compatibleDets() comes back empty and nothing gets appended to the HitPattern.

This only affects secondary hit patterns bookkeeping. The track parameters are not affected so no significant changes in reconstruction are expected.

#### PR validation:

Tested by printing L1 hit patterns of stored, and refitted ALCARECO muon tracks before and after the changes

Before change: 
  ```
  STORED (PixelBarrel layer 1, total 161):
    VALID         124
    MISSING        10
    INACTIVE       27

  REFIT (PixelBarrel layer 1, total 190):
    VALID         123
    MISSING        20
    INACTIVE       47
```

After change:
```
STORED (PixelBarrel layer 1, total 190):
  VALID         124
  MISSING        19
  INACTIVE       47

 REFIT (PixelBarrel layer 1, total 190):
  VALID         123
  MISSING        20
  INACTIVE       47
```
The one differing hit is traced to TrackRefitter needing Fitter='muonSeededFittingSmootherWithOutliersRejectionAndRK' to match muonSeededTracksOutIn's own fitter.

EDIT: 
Backports are
[20.0.X](https://github.com/cms-sw/cmssw/pull/51485) (includes https://github.com/cms-sw/cmssw/pull/51379 backport as well), [17.0.X](https://github.com/cms-sw/cmssw/pull/51496), [16.1.X](https://github.com/cms-sw/cmssw/pull/51497), and [16.0.X](https://github.com/cms-sw/cmssw/pull/51498)
51478.title: Correct compatible det propagation in `setSecondHitPattern`

The assignment of the Phase2Muon type (introduced in #51053) was performed only on tracker muons with a corresponding global muon. Since it must be a general flag, moved the assignment in the main loop, where `outputMuons` collection is completely filled.

51486.author: SegmentLinking
51486.body: The ROCm external package in CMSSW was split, so now the headers are under `rocm-hip`. This was causing LST standalone to fail to compile. The fix is backwards compatible, in case an old CMSSW version is needed.
51486.title: [LST standalone] Fix ROCm compilation

51488.author: pietroGru
51488.body: #### PR description: While looking at the HLT/Vertexing plots in the DQM directory (i.e., `HLT/Vertexing/hltPixelVertices/Alignment/dxyVsEta_pt10` https://cern.ch/7khox), we realized that the horizontal labels in the `TProfile`s produced by the `GeneralPurposeVertexAnalyzer` do indicate the default pT>1 GeV cut also for the plotted quantities related to tracks with pT>10 GeV cut. From conversation with @elusian and @mmasciov, we realized that the pT cut is actually applied (i.e., see [GeneralPurposeVertexAnalyzer.cc#L575-L584](https://github.com/cms-sw/cmssw/blob/CMSSW_20_1_X/Alignment/OfflineValidation/plugins/GeneralPurposeVertexAnalyzer.cc#L575-L584) for the `*_pt10` filled `TH1D`s and `IPMonitoring`s), so this is likely a typo which this PR proposes to fix - by formatting the pT cut in the labels. #### PR validation: The code compiles ok
51488.title: Update `GeneralPurposeVertexAnalyzer` axes labels accordingly to track pT cut

```shell
  File "/cvmfs/cms-ib.cern.ch/sw/riscv64/nweek-02949/fc42_riscv64_gcc15/cms/cmssw/CMSSW_20_1_X_2026-07-09-2300/src/Configuration/EventContent/python/EventContent_cff.py", line 994, in <module>
    from PhysicsTools.PatAlgos.slimming.slimming_cff import MicroEventContent,MicroEventContentMC,MicroEventContentGEN
  File "/cvmfs/cms-ib.cern.ch/sw/riscv64/nweek-02949/fc42_riscv64_gcc15/cms/cmssw/CMSSW_20_1_X_2026-07-09-2300/src/PhysicsTools/PatAlgos/python/slimming/slimming_cff.py", line 3, in <module>
    from PhysicsTools.PatAlgos.slimming.packedPFCandidates_cff import *
  File "/cvmfs/cms-ib.cern.ch/sw/riscv64/nweek-02949/fc42_riscv64_gcc15/cms/cmssw/CMSSW_20_1_X_2026-07-09-2300/src/PhysicsTools/PatAlgos/python/slimming/packedPFCandidates_cff.py", line 5, in <module>
    from CommonTools.ParticleFlow.PFBRECO_cff import pfPileUpPFBRECO, pfNoPileUpPFBRECO
  File "/cvmfs/cms-ib.cern.ch/sw/riscv64/nweek-02949/fc42_riscv64_gcc15/cms/cmssw/CMSSW_20_1_X_2026-07-09-2300/src/CommonTools/ParticleFlow/python/PFBRECO_cff.py", line 4, in <module>
    from RecoParticleFlow.PFProducer.pfLinker_cff import particleFlowPtrs
  File "/cvmfs/cms-ib.cern.ch/sw/riscv64/nweek-02949/fc42_riscv64_gcc15/cms/cmssw/CMSSW_20_1_X_2026-07-09-2300/src/RecoParticleFlow/PFProducer/python/pfLinker_cff.py", line 3, in <module>

ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'RecoParticleFlow.PFProducer.pfLinker_cfi'
``` 

#### PR validation:

Tested under  RISC-V conditions using a docker:
```shell
ssh cmsdev45
mkdir -p /build/`whoami`
cd  /build/`whoami`
wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/cms-sw/cms-bot/refs/heads/master/dockerrun.sh
export SCRAM_ARCH=fc42_riscv64_gcc15
source dockerrun.sh
dockerrun bash
scram l CMSSW_20_1_X
scram p CMSSW_20_1_X_2026-07-09-2300
cd CMSSW_20_1_X_2026-07-09-2300/
cmsenv
``` 
and then using the following workflow:

```shell
cmsDriver.py MinBias_8TeV_pythia8_TuneCUETP8M1_cff --relval 9000,300 -s GEN,SIM -n 10 --conditions auto:run1_mc --beamspot Realistic8TeVCollision --datatier GEN-SIM --eventcontent RAWSIM --customise Validation/Performance/TimeMemorySummary.customiseWithTimeMemorySummary --prefix --fileout file:step1.root --suffix "-j JobReport1.xml " --nThreads 4
```

51508.author: bfonta
51508.body:
Following https://github.com/cms-sw/cmssw/pull/50269, I'm introducing a few more minor changes / additions to `dqm-plot`:

+ Support more labels in the axis scaling function, where 'log' or 'linear' are defined.
+ Support both 1D and 2D histogram division, with correct errors. The current implementation only support 2D divisions.
+ Support an optional horizontal dashed line in the main plot (useful for displaying the line at y=1 for efficiencies, for instance).
+ Re-orient axis labels to be centered and horizontal by default.

All options were locally tested.
51508.title: Minor dqm-plot improvements.

51519.author: raoatifshad
51519.body:
#### PR description:

While going through some clang static analysis logs for DQM/L1TMonitor, I kept seeing the same warning show up over and over (in every file that includes DQMEDAnalyzer.h):
```
  warning: Class 'DQMEDAnalyzerGlobalCache' has publically-accessible
  mutable member 'lumiToken_', this is not allowed [threadsafety.PublicMutableMember]
```
Turns out this isn't really coming from DQM/L1TMonitor at all, it's a warning about DQMEDAnalyzer.h itself, so it shows up in every package that uses it. Two members in DQMEDAnalyzerGlobalCache (lumiToken_ and runToken_) are marked `mutable` and were just sitting there public, which the checker doesn't like because in theory anyone could write to them from outside without any locking.
So I made the members private and move that same locking/check logic into a small method (setTokensOnce) + two read-only getters for the two places that were reading the values directly. Nothing about the locking, timing, or what gets stored changes.it is  just behind a proper interface now.
This PR doesn't touch the design from #27125 or the locking from #27291 - it only makes the tokens private.

Is this solution acceptable?
logs[here](https://cmssdt.cern.ch/SDT/jenkins-artifacts/ib-static-analysis/CMSSW_20_1_X_2026-07-15-2300/el9_amd64_gcc13/build-logs/DQM/L1TMonitor/log.html)
51519.title: [WarningFix]Fix clang thread-safety warning in DQMEDAnalyzer.h

51524.author: felicepantaleo
51524.body:
The v19 HGCal geometry introduces a fourth silicon sensor category (HGCSiliconDetId type 3, HD 200um). 

In the legacy branch of HGCDigitizerBase, `cell.thickness` (1..4) is used directly as the MIP-charge scale of the ZS threshold. This proxy is valid for 120/200/300um (types 0-2) but gives HD 200um a factor 4 instead of 2. 
Measured with single muons at eta 2.3-2.5 (D122 vs D121, no PU):
37% of sim hits in type-3 cells produced no rechit (62.9% efficiency vs 98-100% for all other types), with a three-fold phi pattern following the HD-wafer placement. The fix maps the HD200 factor to 2. Rerunning the same GEN events through the fixed digitizer restores type-3 efficiency to 98.8%.

https://felice.web.cern.ch/orbit/v19_geom_validation_HIGHLIGHTS/21_rechit_efficiency_after_fix.png

The v19 `thicknessCorrection` has 8 silicon entries (4 per section) but `deltasi_index_regemfac` remained 3, so all CE-H silicon rechits were misbinned (CE-H HD120 read the CE-E HD200 slot: +12% energy and wrong sigma-noise). The layer cluster additionally copy their constants from HGCalRecHit at import time, before era modifiers apply, so on v19 they ran with pre-v19 6-slot arrays and sent CE-H HD200 hits to the scintillator threshold slot. This adds `deltasi_index_regemfac = 4` to the v19 rechit
modifier and explicit v19 overrides (8 thickness slots, v19 fCPerMIP, noises, thickness corrections) for the EE/HSi/HSci cluster producers.
51524.title: Fix HGCal HD 200um (type 3) handling in legacy digitizer ZS and v19 local reco constants

51527.author: SegmentLinking
51527.body:
#### PR description:

This should fix the issue reported in https://github.com/cms-sw/cmssw/issues/51512.

#### PR validation:

The workflow runs successfully for `34634.713` using the GPU accelerator.

#### If this PR is a backport please specify the original PR and why you need to backport that PR. If this PR will be backported please specify to which release cycle the backport is meant for:

No

FYI @slava77 @mmasciov
51527.title: Fix SerialSync sequence for Phase-2 offline LST-seeded mkFit

51529.author: felicepantaleo
51529.body:
The five global associator producers mutated a shared RecHitTools member via setGeometry() inside const produce(), racing across streams and giving an ASAN double-free on the RecHitTools internal vector buffer. 

fixes https://github.com/cms-sw/cmssw/issues/51380
51529.title: Build RecHitTools per event to fix cross-stream double-free

51532.author: thomreis
51532.body:
#### PR description:

Do not run on ECAL endcap collections for the ECAL Phase 2 DQM tasks (Cluster, Energy, Timing). This PR removes warning messages about missing collections when running the ECAL DQM with the ECAL development era modifier (`phase2_ecal_devel`).

Additionally, the ECAL Phase 2 collections are added to the RECO event content with the ECAL development modifiers.

No changes to outputs are expected.

#### PR validation:

The warning messages are removed in WF 34402.612
51532.title: Fix ECAL DQM warnings about missing endcap collections in Phase 2
